title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering focus upward from line-by-line code writing to system design. She contends that traditional engineering disciplines like decomposition, state management, and systems thinking remain central to building agentic systems, and that developer value migrates to the architectural level rather than disappearing.
writeup: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents move engineering focus up from line-by-line writing to system design. The talk holds that core engineering disciplines, decomposition, separation of concerns, state and context management, and systems thinking, stay central to building agentic and multi-agent systems. The central takeaway: developer value migrates to the architectural level rather than disappearing, with the human work shifting toward designing how systems fit together.
